title: Real-estate weekly listing stills package — 6 scenes from 5-bracket RAW: masked window-pull HDR grade, straightened verticals, one shared gallery preset, print + web JPEGs, plus Illustrator data-merged 'just-listed' rider labels
one_line_ask: Take 5 bracketed exposures per scene (6 scenes), blend to a natural-HDR look via fine tonal grading, straighten verticals / correct perspective, mask and recover blown windows (window-pull), open interiors while keeping clean neutral whites, calm over-green lawns / over-blue skies, lock one consistent gallery preset across all 6 for a cohesive set, export print-resolution + web/portal JPEGs of each, then data-merge a small batch of Illustrator 'just-listed' rider / yard-sign labels (address, beds/baths/sqft, price, agent) from the listing CSV using a user-authored .ai Variables template.
deliverables: 6 final natural-HDR scene stills with balanced exposure, recovered window highlights, opened shadows, clean neutral whites and corrected vertical perspective
Straight verticals / corrected geometry on every interior and exterior frame
Masked window-pull on each window-bearing interior so exterior detail reads instead of blowing out
A single shared gallery 'look' (one Lightroom preset) applied across all 6 so the set is cohesive
Print-ready high-resolution JPEG export of every scene (full-res, ~300dpi target)
Web/portal-optimized JPEG export of every scene (portal long-edge, sRGB)
A batch of Illustrator-merged 'just-listed' rider / yard-sign labels (address, beds/baths/sqft, price, agent name+phone) rendered to print PDF + PNG from the listing CSV
A local contact-sheet/gallery proof PDF laying out all 6 graded scenes for client sign-off
source: Upwork db-6004 'Real Estate Photo Editor Needed for Bracketed RAW Interior & Exterior Images' (verbatim-anchored in pipeline/data/adobe.db items.id=6004): "each final image is normally captured using 5 RAW bracketed exposures ... Natural and realistic, not over-edited; Clean whites and balanced colors; Good window recovery when possible; Straight verticals and corrected perspective; Bright but still realistic interiors; Proper contrast and sharpness; Consistent look across the full gallery." Rider-label sub-deliverable is a standard real-estate listing-collateral add-on.
